commit.message:
fix: per-inner-step backward to prevent OOM in Phase B/C

Each inner step now calls backward_step immediately with scaled loss
(loss / inner_steps) instead of accumulating all N computation graphs
into a single tensor before backward. This prevents holding 8x forward
graphs in VRAM simultaneously.

Gradient semantics: 8 sequential AdamW updates with 1/8-scaled gradients,
rather than one update with full accumulated gradient. Mathematically
equivalent for SGD; near-equivalent for Adam in practice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
EXP-1559: Meal detection sensitivity analysis (3 configs)

Compare three meal detection configurations across 11 patients:
  A (Census):  ≥5g carbs, 30-min gap → 4,072 meals, quality 0.923
  B (Medium):  ≥5g carbs, 90-min gap → 3,272 meals, quality 0.973
  C (Therapy): ≥18g carbs, 90-min gap → 2,632 meals, quality 0.979

Key findings:
- 90-min hysteresis removes 20% of meals, boosts quality significantly
- ≥18g threshold removes 35% total but achieves 99.2% excellent grade
- Patient impact varies: a loses 72%, e loses only 7%
- Excursion-carb relationship stable across configs (~85 mg/dL for ≥50g)

Parameterized detect_meal_windows() with min_carbs and cluster_gap kwargs.
Added fig8_meal_sensitivity.png visualization (4-panel comparison).

Refactor SetToken to clear cache before writing to avoid 'record already exists'

Port of Bertverbeek4PS/bc2adls#497: Replace TryWriteToCache fallback pattern with
a simpler ClearCache() + WriteToCache() approach. Deleting existing entries first
ensures IsolatedStorage.Set always performs an INSERT on a clean state, which is
safe on both SaaS and On-Premises.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Skip Start_Disposed_ThrowsObjectDisposedException on Apple mobile platforms (#126655)

## Fix

Adds `[SkipOnPlatform(TestPlatforms.iOS | TestPlatforms.tvOS |
TestPlatforms.MacCatalyst)]` to
`Start_Disposed_ThrowsObjectDisposedException`.

## Why

`Process.Start()` on iOS/tvOS/MacCatalyst throws
`PlatformNotSupportedException` (process launching is unsupported)
**before** checking disposal state. The test expects
`ObjectDisposedException` which never fires, causing a deterministic
failure across **7 legs** in every `runtime-extra-platforms` rolling
build.

This follows the same `[SkipOnPlatform]` pattern used by 20+ other tests
in the same file.

Fixes #126654

Add build/test metrics dashboard, fix conflict detector false positives, fix PM review contradictions

- BuildTestMetrics: new service with 18 named metrics (build.*, test.*, rework.*, commits.*)
- AgentStateStore: added GetAggregateMetricsAsync, GetMetricsByAgentAsync query helpers
- EngineerAgentBase: wired metrics recording at all decision points (build attempts,
  successes, failures, fix attempts, regenerations, blocked commits, test runs, rework)
- All engineer subclasses (Senior, Junior, Principal) + TestEngineerAgent: accept optional
  BuildTestMetrics parameter via constructor
- Metrics.razor: 4 new dashboard sections (Build Health, Test Health, Rework Health,
  Commit Quality) with success rate calculations and color-coded indicators
- Program.cs: registered BuildTestMetrics as singleton

Bug fixes:
- ConflictDetector: was comparing filenames only, causing false positive warnings when
  the same filename exists in different directories (e.g., src/Pages/Index.razor vs
  Pages/Index.razor). Now uses IsSuspiciousDuplicate() that checks path suffix overlap
  and parent directory similarity.
- PullRequestWorkflow.ApproveAndMaybeMergeAsync: reason parameter was ignored, approval
  comments now include the review rationale.
- ProgramManagerAgent + PrincipalEngineerAgent: AI response text contamination caused
  both CHANGES REQUESTED and APPROVED to appear in the same comment. Now strips standalone
  decision keyword lines from reviewBody before posting.

commit.message:
security: rate-limit gossip spam — disconnect on collateral hijack, track AV3 cycling violations

- Add RegistryError::IpCyclingRejected distinct from InvalidCollateral
- Return IpCyclingRejected from AV3 IP-cycling lockout path so violations
  can be tracked separately from other InvalidCollateral rejections
- Propagate disconnect signal from CollateralAlreadyLocked handlers in
  both paid-tier and free-tier announcement paths: record_severe_violation
  return value was previously discarded, leaving the connection alive after
  the 1-hour ban was placed
- Add IpCyclingRejected match arms in both handlers: calls record_violation
  (3-strike escalating ban) and disconnects when should_ban returns true

Before: 50.28.106.227 and 133.18.180.117 could spam 15+ gossip rejections
per 30s window indefinitely — violation was recorded but connection stayed
alive and blacklist check on messages was not applied to active connections.
After: first CollateralAlreadyLocked → 1h ban + disconnect; third AV3
cycling rejection → 1min ban + disconnect.

commit.message:
perf: escape-free string rendering fast path with bulk copy (#678)

## Motivation

`visitNonNullString` delegates to `upickle.core.RenderUtils.escapeChar`
for every string, which processes each character one-by-one — checking
for control chars, quotes, and backslashes, then appending. In typical
Jsonnet output, the vast majority of strings contain no characters that
need escaping. Processing these clean strings character-by-character is
unnecessary overhead.

## Key Design Decision

Add a **pre-scan gate** before calling `escapeChar`. This is NOT a
reimplementation of `escapeChar` — it's a fast check that determines
whether `escapeChar` needs to be called at all:

1. **Quick scan**: Loop through string chars checking `c < 32 || c ==
'"' || c == '\\'`
2. **If clean** (common case): Skip `escapeChar` entirely, bulk-copy via
`String.getChars()` with surrounding quotes
3. **If dirty**: Fall through to existing `escapeChar` (unchanged)

## Modification

**`sjsonnet/src/sjsonnet/BaseCharRenderer.scala`** —
`visitNonNullString`:
- Added `String` type check and `!escapeUnicode` guard
- Pre-scan loop: tight `while` checking for escape-requiring chars
- Fast path: `elemBuilder.ensureLength(len + 2)` → `appendUnsafe('"')` →
`String.getChars()` bulk copy → `appendUnsafe('"')`
- Slow path: original `upickle.core.RenderUtils.escapeChar` call
(unchanged)
- `CharSequence` fallback: original behavior (unchanged)

## Benchmark Results

### JMH — Full Suite (35 benchmarks, 1+1 warmup)

No regressions detected. All benchmarks within noise margin.

### Note

On the JVM, the JIT compiler can partially optimize `escapeChar`'s
per-character loop. The pre-scan gate's primary value is:
1. **Avoiding function call overhead**: `escapeChar` is an external
library call (`upickle`) that the JIT may not fully inline
2. **Enabling bulk copy**: `String.getChars()` is a JVM intrinsic
(`System.arraycopy`)
3. **Scala Native**: No JIT — the pre-scan + bulk copy path is
significantly faster than per-character processing

## Analysis

- **Correctness**: The pre-scan checks the exact same conditions as
`escapeChar` (`c < 32`, `'"'`, `'\\'`). If any char needs escaping, we
fall through to `escapeChar` unchanged.
- **`escapeUnicode` guard**: When `escapeUnicode = true`, additional
chars need escaping (non-ASCII). We skip the fast path in this case and
always use `escapeChar`.
- **Common case**: In typical Jsonnet config output, >95% of strings are
ASCII with no control chars, quotes, or backslashes — the fast path
handles these.

## References

- `upickle.core.RenderUtils.escapeChar`:
[source](https://github.com/com-lihaoyi/upickle/blob/87e0b24b8c811e174ebd680839e4edf1e62abe71/upickle/core/src/upickle/core/RenderUtils.scala)
- `String.getChars`: JVM intrinsic backed by `System.arraycopy`

## Result

Pre-scan gate for escape-free string rendering. Skips per-character
`escapeChar` processing for clean strings (the common case). No
regressions. Benefits Scala Native and reduces function call overhead.

commit.message:
feat: improve interactive tutorial with progress tracking, tips, and keyboard nav (#576)

## Summary

Enhances the existing interactive tutorial (from PR #178) with better
UX, progress tracking, and visual polish.

## Changes

### Tutorial Overlay
- **Step progress dots** — visual dots showing current position within a
chapter (active/done/upcoming)
- **Overall progress bar** — thin bar at the bottom tracking global step
position
- **Keyboard navigation** — Arrow keys (left/right) to navigate, Escape
to close
- **Slide animations** — left/right transition between steps
- **Tip callouts** — contextual hints with lightbulb icon and
accent-colored left border
- **Better visual hierarchy** — chapter name in accent color, chapter
counter, SVG close button

### Tutorial Page
- **Overall progress section** — gradient progress bar with chapter
completion count
- **Numbered chapter cards** — chapter numbers in circular badges
- **Smart "Continue Tour" button** — resumes from first incomplete
chapter instead of restarting
- **"Replay Full Tour"** — shown when all chapters are completed
- **Keyboard shortcut hint** — footer note about arrow keys and Escape
- **Responsive layout** — single-column grid on mobile (\<640px)

### First-Launch Experience
- **Tutorial nudge banner** — shows on empty dashboard for new users
- **Dismissible** — persisted via `HasSeenTutorialPrompt` in UiState

### Model / Service
- **`TutorialStep.Tip`** — optional contextual hint property
- **`TutorialService` progress properties** — `TotalChapters`,
`TotalSteps`, `GlobalStepIndex`, `OverallProgressPercent`,
`CompletedChapterCount`
- **`UiState.HasSeenTutorialPrompt`** — persists nudge dismissal

### Tests
- 7 new tests: `TotalChapters`, `TotalSteps`, `GlobalStepIndex`,
`OverallProgressPercent` (zero/partial/full), `CompletedChapterCount`,
`TipsAreNotEmpty`
- All 3331 tests pass (0 failures)

[rocshmem] functional_tests/driver.sh: add feature to specify custom test config (#4700)

## Motivation

It is not currently possible to use the functional test driver script
to run a single test with a custom configuration.
This PR adds this feature, which can be used as follows:
```Shell
# rocshmem_functional_driver.sh <test_executable> "<test_name> <ranks> <workgroups> <threads> [max_msg_size]" <log_dir>
cd rocshmem_build_dir
mkdir functional_test_logs
# putnbi test with 2 PEs, 32 workgroups, 256 threads per workgroup, and up to 512B per message
./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_driver.sh ./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_tests "putnbi 2 32 256 512" functional_test_logs
less functional_test_logs/putnbi_n2_w32_z256_512B.log
# amo_finc test with 2 PEs, 1 workgroup, and 1024 threads per workgroup
./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_driver.sh ./tests/functional_tests/rocshmem_functional_tests "amo_finc 2 1 1024" functional_test_logs
less functional_test_logs/amo_finc_n2_w1_z1024.log
```

## Technical Details

Extend the driver script to handle arbitrary single test configurations
in the same format as used for input to the ExecTest script function:
* If `$TEST` doesn't match any preset,
  `$TEST` is parsed into a bash array `TEST_OPTS=($TEST)`;
  this is implicitly split at whitespace boundary by bash
* Index 0 is the test name, `NAME=${TEST_OPTS[0]}`
* If the length of the array is 4 or 5 then indices 1–4 are parsed
  as ranks, workgroups, threads, and max message size
* Otherwise the same static default configuration as before is used:
  2 ranks, 1 workgroup, 1 thread, and a max message size of 8B

## JIRA ID

AIROCSHMEM-356

## Test Plan

Run a variety of test configurations using the new custom test feature.

## Test Result

Feature has been in internal use for a few months with no apparent
issues or regressions.

- [x] Look over the contributing guidelines at
https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm/blob/develop/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests.

commit.message:
fix: prevent negative USD values in ScenarioEditor

Four defensive fixes for the %/USD mode toggle:

1. Disable USD/PLN toggle when no reference price — prevents toggling
   before stock data loads, which left percentage values in fixed-mode
   fields (root cause of negative dollar amounts and overflow).

2. Early return in toggle functions when currentPrice/Rate <= 0 —
   belt-and-suspenders guard matching the disabled button state.

3. Clamp toDelta output to >= -100% — a stock/FX can never drop more
   than 100%, so any input producing a delta below -100 is capped.
   This prevents impossible negative end values in calculations.

4. Add min=0 on fixed-mode inputs — browser validation hint that
   absolute prices/rates cannot be negative.

commit.message:
Fix mmol/L ISF handling: add unit conversion and auto-detection

Patient a uses mmol/L for ISF (value=2.7 ≈ 48.6 mg/dL). Production
code was reading ISF values raw without unit conversion, causing
incorrect metabolic flux, clinical ISF comparisons, and settings advice.

Changes:
- types.py: Add 'units' field to PatientProfile, isf_mgdl() method
  with auto-detection (all values < 15 → mmol/L), MMOL_TO_MGDL constant
- metabolic_engine.py: Use profile.isf_mgdl() instead of raw schedule
- clinical_rules.py: Use profile.isf_mgdl() for profile ISF extraction
- settings_advisor.py: Use profile.isf_mgdl() in both ISF advice paths
- meal_detector.py: Use profile.isf_mgdl() for meal sizing
- test_production.py: 8 new tests (72 total): explicit mmol/L conversion,
  auto-detection, sensitivity key, CR unaffected, pipeline end-to-end

The auto-detection matches the research heuristic from
exp_metabolic_flux.py:166 (if isf < 15: isf *= 18.0182).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix infinite scroll loading all pages without user scrolling

Two issues caused the InfiniteScrolling component to load all pages
immediately on page load instead of waiting for user scroll:

1. findClosestScrollContainer returned <main class='main-content'>
   which has overflow-y:auto but grows to fit all content (height
   equals scrollHeight). The IntersectionObserver used this as its
   root, so the indicator was always 'visible' within the
   non-scrolling container. Fixed by also checking that
   scrollHeight > clientHeight before selecting a scroll container.

2. onNewItems (which re-observes the scroll indicator) was called
   from LoadMoreItems before Blazor had rendered new items to the
   DOM. Moved the onNewItems call to OnAfterRenderAsync so the
   observer re-observes only after the DOM is updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
